summary: Remove the `frt_escalated_at` fallback from on-call gap detection. SLA-escalation time is when the breach timer fired, not when an agent responded; using it as a fallback excluded tickets that escalated without ever being responded to from the gap report.
reason: Greptile round-4 P1 on PR #533. Hand-authored novel-command logic in `oncall-gap`: tickets with no `first_responded_at` should fall into the `time.Since(created)` branch (which correctly measures elapsed-since-creation against `--ack-minutes`). The earlier code instead read `frt_escalated_at` as a response timestamp, producing false negatives whenever the SLA escalation fell inside the ack window.
validated_outcome: Tickets that were never responded to now appear in the gap report when their elapsed-since-creation exceeds `--ack-minutes`, regardless of whether the FRT SLA escalation fired in the meantime.
